notes: searches: ~0, clusters: 0. Delta output used (not full file): 125 claims with several long verbatim KJV blocks make full reproduction a token/frozen-field risk; parent authorized delta fallback. All checkable claims resolved via common-knowledge fast-path: (a) 46 KJV Scripture quotations checked for fidelity against the standard KJV text (all faithful; GC-39-077 and GC-39-121 render 'Jehovah'/'Lord Jehovah' for KJV 'the LORD', a translation variant with meaning unchanged, still supported); (b) ~25 event/biographical claims are faithful retellings of named biblical passages (faithful-retelling convention); (c) GC-39-068 Piedmont Waldenses mountain refuge = textbook history, consistent with earlier chapters' corpus; GC-39-019 arithmetic (~AD 31 -> 1911 rounds to 'eighteen hundred years'); GC-39-009 Jerusalem AD 70 benchmark = textbook history. Theological/eschatological claims marked unverifiable per epistemic rule 5. No doctrinally-central escalation cluster is engaged in a checkable way (GC-39-062 Sabbath/Sunday is a predictive/theological statement, not a checkable historical assertion). Control sample (3 false-flagged): GC-39-016, GC-39-062, GC-39-118 - all confirmed genuinely theological, no smuggled fact.

GC-39-001.rationale: [common_knowledge] Faithfully renders KJV Daniel 12:1; wording matches the standard text (opening 'And' dropped, meaning unchanged).

GC-39-002.rationale: Prophetic/theological interpretation of Daniel 12:1 as a future time of trouble; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-003.rationale: Eschatological assertion about the close of the third angel's message and cessation of mercy; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-004.rationale: Theological assertion about the 'latter rain' preparation of God's people; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-005.rationale: [common_knowledge] The quoted words 'It is done' faithfully render KJV Revelation 16:17; the surrounding narration is authorial framing.

GC-39-007.rationale: Theological assertion about the close of Christ's intercession and blotting out of sins; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-008.rationale: Theological assertion about removal of restraint and Satan's control of the impenitent; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-009.rationale: [common_knowledge] The comparative benchmark - the historical destruction of Jerusalem (AD 70) as a catastrophe of exceptional severity - is textbook history (cf. GC ch.1/ch.4). The future-ruin portion is an unverifiable prediction, but the historical anchor used is sound.

GC-39-010.rationale: [common_knowledge] Faithful retelling of Exodus 12 (death of the firstborn); the 'destroying angel' framing follows Exodus 12:23 ('the destroyer'), a standard reading.

GC-39-011.rationale: [common_knowledge] Faithful retelling of 2 Samuel 24 / 1 Chronicles 21 (David's census, the plague/angel, ~70,000 dead).

GC-39-012.rationale: Theological assertion about destructive power exercised by evil angels when God permits; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-013.rationale: Interpretive/eschatological assertion that law-keepers will be blamed for end-time disasters; a future-conditional prediction, not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-014.rationale: Theological assertion about Satan intensifying persecution; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-015.rationale: [common_knowledge] Historically sound: temple sacrificial worship continued in Jerusalem after the crucifixion until the temple's destruction in AD 70; the characterization of priesthood/people echoes the Acts narrative. Textbook history.

GC-39-016.rationale: Control-sample recheck (false-flagged). Confirmed genuinely theological: assertion that God's presence was 'finally withdrawn' from the Jewish nation is a divine-agency claim, not a checkable historical fact. No smuggled empirical assertion. Not checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-017.rationale: Eschatological assertion about the unperceived pronouncement of final judgment; not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-018.rationale: Predictive/theological assertion about a future Sabbath-Sunday enforcement crisis; a future-conditional prophecy, not empirically checkable (rule 5).

GC-39-019.rationale: [common_knowledge] Simple arithmetic from the 1888/1911 writing: the trial of Christ (~AD 31-33) is roughly eighteen hundred years before the time of writing; 'eighteen hundred years ago' is accurate to the century.

GC-39-068.rationale: [common_knowledge] Historically sound: the Waldensian Christians of the Piedmont valleys used the high alpine places as refuges and worship sanctuaries during persecution - a well-documented commonplace, consistent with earlier chapters' Waldensian corpus. The 'munitions of rocks' phrase echoes Isaiah 33:16.
